review:
The crypto market enters the week of June 14 in a confirmed bear regime with a composite signal of no action, a configuration that has not changed. Fear and Greed sits at 18, deep in extreme fear territory, yet the framework finds no actionable dislocation - meaning derivatives stress and fundamental deterioration are broadly aligned rather than diverging. The slight bounce over the past eight days, with BTC up 4.2 percent and SOL up 7.2 percent, looks more like oversold noise than regime change. Funding rates remain negative on a z-score basis, consistent with a market where marginal participants are leaning short or hedged rather than positioned for recovery.

The one-month and one-quarter windows tell a coherent and persistent story. The phase distribution shows an unbroken 68-day bear regime with no transitional sessions. The average BTC composite score of roughly 43 over both windows is below neutral and has not been lifting, and the action distribution across the quarter shows only six buy signals against 62 no-action days. This is not a regime in flux; it is a regime that is consolidating downward pressure. The ETH/BTC z-score averaging negative 1.3 over the month confirms that institutional rotation into Ethereum has not materialized, and the SOL/BTC z of negative 1.58 over the same period signals that risk appetite within crypto remains compressed toward the quality end, such as it is.

YTD returns through mid-June reflect a bear that has been orderly rather than panicked. BTC is down roughly 11 percent, ETH down 23 percent, and SOL down 26 percent from the dataset start. The ETH/BTC underperformance is structurally significant, suggesting the post-halving cycle has not produced the altcoin rotation that characterized 2020 to 2021. If we are 14 months past the April 2024 halving, the absence of a risk-on rotation into ETH and SOL is a bearish structural signal for cycle believers.

Cross-asset context is notably calm. The equity market quadrant registers as business as usual, with VIX at the 43rd percentile of its ten-year range and credit spreads untroubled. This means crypto stress is idiosyncratic rather than part of a systemic risk-off. The Tepper-style dislocation setup - fear in one market against calm in another - is present in structure but the framework does not yet flag it as actionable. What would change the signal is a sustained lift in the BTC composite score toward 55 or above, a reversion of ETH/BTC and SOL/BTC z-scores toward zero, and F&
